t_ch: 嘿嘿，这么一想，我还是个挺不可或缺的人物呢……
t_en: Hee-hee, when I think about it, I'm still a pretty important staff member around here...

t_ch: 你怎么看待这里的皮套人？
t_en: What do you think of the costumed people here?

t_ch: 你是指扮演动漫形象的同事？我敬重他们，就像我敬重每一个创作者那样。
t_en: You mean my coworkers playing the cartoon characters? I really respect them, just like I admire every creator.

t_ch: 不管在现实还是梦境，每部能被人观赏的作品都是千万个系统时辛勤劳动的结晶。
t_en: Whether in reality or in the Dreamscape, every work that manages to finally reach the audience is the fruition of tens of millions of system hours of blood, sweat, and tears.

t_ch: 而想要构建出我们喜爱的世界，除了创意的提供者以外，也离不开将创意付诸实际的执行者。
t_en: And to create a world we all love, we can't forget the people who helped execute the idea as well as those who provided the idea.

t_ch: 每当看见钟表小子和伙伴们在荧幕上舞蹈，我都要由衷地发出赞叹——大家居然能将那些虚拟角色演绎得如此惟妙惟肖。
t_en: Every time I see Clockie and his companions dance on the screen, I can't help but marvel in earnest at how everyone brought those fictional characters so vividly to life.

t_ch: 希望有朝一日我也能参与到动画创作中去……
t_en: I hope that one day I can also play a part in animation production...

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's it. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 再见，希望你在这里玩得开心。
t_en: Goodbye, and I hope you have fun here.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's it. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 再见，希望你在这里玩得开心。
t_en: Goodbye, and I hope you have fun here.

t_ch: 请问您有什么需要帮忙的吗？
t_en: Do you need help with anything?

t_ch: …请见谅，我压力大的时候总是容易喜怒无常。毕竟这里的工作并不轻松。
t_en: ...I apologize. My mood swings get erratic when I'm stressed out. The work here isn't exactly a walk in the park.

t_ch: 那你还来做志愿者？
t_en: Why did you volunteer then?

t_ch: 这是个夕阳产业，趁早转行吧。
t_en: It's a dying industry. Better jump ship early.

t_ch: 哈哈，多谢关心，不过辞职还是算了。
t_en: Haha, thank you for your concern, but I'm not planning on leaving.

t_ch: 虽然确实会有些劳累…但当工作结束后、我对着日历回想每天的经历时，总觉得一切努力都是有意义的。
t_en: It's quite tiring here... but when work is over, and I think back on my experiences of the day while staring at the calendar, I always feel that all that hard work is worth it.

t_ch: 因为我、以及我的同事们，一直在用双手筑就全新的东西。
t_en: Because I, and my coworkers, are building something totally new with our own hands.

t_ch: 我们所做的基础工作，会切实地为克劳克影城提供帮助。指不定下一部钟表小子电影的片尾名单里就有我呢。
t_en: All the groundwork we do will realistically help Clock Studios Theme Park. Who knows, I might be in the ending credits in the next Clockie movie.

t_ch: 艾薇拉的脸上重拾了喜悦与激情。她对你露出一个笑容，用元气满满的声音开了口——
t_en: Elvira's face is marked with joy and passion yet again. She smiles at you, and musters her gusto as she says:

t_ch: 那么现在，请问您有什么需要帮忙的吗？
t_en: Now, is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 还有很多事情要做…加油干吧。
t_en: There's much more to do... gotta work harder!

t_ch: 哎呀，我怎么还在这说闲话，明明有很多事情没做呢……
t_en: Gah, what am I doing here still chatting away. I still get lots of things to do...

t_ch: 门口的导览牌要修缮，游戏机里的代币要更换，动漫人物的扮演者们也需要慰问一下……
t_en: The signboard at the entrance needs fixing, the tokens in the arcade machines need changing, and there are cartoon character actors who need attending to...

t_ch: 艾薇拉陷入了沉默。某种一直萦绕着她的热忱消失了，取而代之的是工作最本真的情感——枯燥。
t_en: Elvira falls into a deep silence. A certain kind of enthusiasm that surrounded her has disappeared, replaced by the most fundamental emotion associated with work: Monotony.

t_ch: 哪怕梦想变成了工作，也终究是一份工作。
t_en: Even if one manages to work at a dream job, in the end, it's just that: A job.

t_ch: 唉，难道属于动画片的年代真的已经过去了吗？
t_en: *sigh* Is the age of cartoons truly over?

t_ch: 唉，这年头，喜欢动画片的人越来越少了……
t_en: *sigh* There are fewer and fewer people who like cartoons these days...

t_ch: 曾经跟我一起追剧的好友们早就退坑了。大家都说自己有很多事情要忙，不能再把时间花在这些没用的东西上。
t_en: The friends who used to binge-watch the series with me quit way early on. Everyone says they've got a lot of stuff to do, and have no time to spend on such useless activities.

t_ch: 我知道啊，现实的重力总是会打破一切幻想，把人们从天空拽回地面。我早就知道这个道理，只是无力改变……
t_en: I know, the gravity of reality always shatters all fantasies, dragging them from the sky back to earth. I knew this a long time ago, but I can't do anything to change it...

t_ch: 不过我相信，终有一天，等到人们被枯燥的现实折磨得遍体鳞伤…他们一定会重新对美好的事物投去目光。
t_en: But I believe that one day, when people are battered by the monotony of reality... They will once again cast their eyes on happier things.

t_ch: 我愿意等待那天的到来…换句话说，除了等待以外，我还能做些什么呢？
t_en: I'm willing to wait for that day to come... Rather, what else can I do besides wait for it?

t_ch: 唉，这年头，喜欢动画片的人越来越少了……
t_en: *sigh* There are fewer and fewer people who like cartoons these days...

t_ch: 曾经跟我一起追剧的好友们早就退坑了。大家都说自己有很多事情要忙，不能再把时间花在这些没用的东西上。
t_en: The friends who used to binge-watch the series with me quit way early on. Everyone says they've got a lot of stuff to do, and have no time to spend on such useless activities.

t_ch: 我知道啊，现实的重力总是会打破一切幻想，把人们从天空拽回地面。我早就知道这个道理，只是无力改变……
t_en: I know, the gravity of reality always shatters all fantasies, dragging them from the sky back to earth. I knew this a long time ago, but I can't do anything to change it...

t_ch: 不过我相信，终有一天，等到人们被枯燥的现实折磨得遍体鳞伤…他们一定会重新对美好的事物投去目光。
t_en: But I believe that one day, when people are battered by the monotony of reality... They will once again cast their eyes on happier things.

t_ch: 我愿意等待那天的到来…换句话说，除了等待以外，我还能做些什么呢？
t_en: I'm willing to wait for that day to come... Rather, what else can I do besides wait for it?

t_ch: 为什么，为什么总有人冒出来破坏我的梦想？！
t_en: Why, why are people always popping out to ruin my dream!?

t_ch: 啊啊啊，到底什么时候才能加入家族、见到真正的钟表小子啊？
t_en: Aaahhh, when will I get to join The Family and see the real Clockie?

t_ch: 加入家族？
t_en: Join The Family?

t_ch: 真正的钟表小子？
t_en: The real Clockie?

t_ch: 是啊，苜蓿草家的人告诉我，只要成为「家人」，就能接触到真正的钟表小子的秘密。
t_en: Yeah, the Alfalfas told me that I'd get to know the real Clockie's secret if I became a Family member.

t_ch: 之前他们确实派人来邀请过我，那会儿我都以为自己要成功了——结果他们一听我是为了钟表小子才加入家族，就又把我退了回来。
t_en: They actually did send someone to invite me previously, and I thought I was this close to succeeding... In the end they got wind that I was only joining The Family for Clockie, and rejected me.

t_ch: 看他们那表情，就好像我说要往松饼里加两片月亮似的…至不至于啊？！
t_en: From their looks of disdain, it's like I said I wanted to add two slices of the moon to my waffle or something... Was it really that bad a desire!?

t_ch: 到底是谁劈了美梦小镇影院的屏幕？知不知道维护它需要多高的成本啊！
t_en: Who was it who cleaved Dreamville Cinema's screen in two? Do you know how much it takes to maintain it!

t_ch: 原本我最大的娱乐就是看大屏幕上轮播的钟表小子动画——没想到现在就连这点消遣都被剥夺了！对它的破坏简直是对美梦的亵渎！
t_en: Initially, my greatest joy was to watch Clockie's cartoon reruns on the big screen. Now even this small slice of happiness has been ripped away from me! Its destruction is a desecration of a beautiful dream!

t_ch: 为什么在梦里也要打打杀杀，到底是谁这么想不开？就不能老老实实地坐下来欣赏艺术吗！
t_en: Why must violence be committed even in dreams? What're these people angry about? Why can't they just sit back and enjoy art!

t_ch: 怎么办啊…要不干脆去算一卦？或者去大街上抓个弹簧荷官？
t_en: What do I do... How about I just go get my fortune told? Or should I head to the main street to catch a Spring Loader?

t_ch: 算什么卦?
t_en: Get your fortune told?

t_ch: 算事业啊，我正巧认识个仙舟来的狐人，算得可准了，就是收费有点贵……
t_en: For my job. I coincidentally ran into a foxian from Xianzhou, and she's the real deal. Just that her fee is a bit dear...

t_ch: 弹簧荷官？
t_en: Spring Loader?

t_ch: 对啊，让它给我丢个骰子，根据点数决定到底怎么选……
t_en: Yeah, get it to throw a die for me, and make my choice from the number thrown...

t_ch: 嘿…等等！相遇即是有缘，不如你来给我些建议吧？我就当你同意了啊——
t_en: Hey... Wait! It's destiny that we encountered each other. How about you give me a couple of suggestions? I'll take your silence as consent...

t_ch: 我问你，如果有两个选项摆在你面前：要么当个无忧无虑的高级打工人，要么为了自由自在地追求梦想而劳碌奔波，你会选哪个？
t_en: Lemme ask, if you had two choices in front of you: To be a carefree and highly paid employee, or chase your dream through hardships in the name of freedom. Which would you choose?

t_ch: 高级打工人挺好的。
t_en: A highly paid employee sounds good.

t_ch: 哈，你也这么觉得吗…也对…人嘛，总得学会活得现实点儿。
t_en: Ha, you feel that way too... That's true... We all have to learn to be more realistic about our life goals.

t_ch: 当然是自由万岁啦！
t_en: Freedom forever, obviously!

t_ch: 哈，你也这么觉得吗…果然…即便美梦早已被金钱锈蚀，人也不该放弃自己的追求……
t_en: Ha, you feel that way too... That's right... Even if dreams have long been corrupted by money, we should never abandon our pursuits...

t_ch: 就不能当条咸鱼吗？
t_en: Can't I just be an average lazy bum?

t_ch: …你能不能有点追求？算了，就当我没问过吧。
t_en: ...Can you at least have a little ambition? Meh, forget I ever asked.

t_ch: 谢谢你的帮助，但这件事情对我而言至关重要，我还得再好好考虑一下……
t_en: Thanks for your help, but this matter is of utmost importance to me. I still have to ponder on it properly...

t_ch: 要聊聊吗？
t_en: Do you need to talk?

t_ch: 行啊，反正我一时半会儿也做不出决定……
t_en: Sure, not like I can make a decision on the fly anyway...

t_ch: 你是哪种打工人？
t_en: So what kind of employee are you?

t_ch: 我…？我是个梦泡艺术家。哈…很神奇对不对，「艺术家」和「打工人」这两个词看起来明明如此不相干……
t_en: Me...? I'm a dream bubble artist. Ha... Amazing, isn't it? When these two words "artist" and "employee" do not seem related at all...

t_ch: 可实际上，如果失去了资本的支持与运作，所谓的「艺术」也不过是滞销的商品罢了。
t_en: But in reality, if we lost the support and management from corporations, our so-called "art" would be nothing but unsalable commodities.

t_ch: 你的梦想是什么？
t_en: What is your dream?

t_ch: 梦想啊…我的梦想是能有这么一天——当人们谈论起我的作品时，他们不谈销量、不论票房，而是认真解读、理性探讨，去挖掘、去理解作品的内容与内涵，以及隐藏在背后的真正的「我」……
t_en: Dream... My dream is to have such a day — when people talk about my works, they would not deliberate its sales nor discuss its box office, but to seriously analyze and rationally study it, to delve into it and understand its content and meaning, and to find the true "me" hidden within...

t_ch: 不过我也知道，这只是痴人说梦罢了，哈哈。
t_en: But I also know that's just the talk of a lunatic dreamer, haha.

t_ch: 不聊了。
t_en: That's it for today.

t_ch: 没问题，正好我也需要一些时间来沉淀我的思绪。
t_en: It's fine. I also need some time to percolate on my decision.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's it. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 要不抛个硬币试试？算了，这也太过随便了……
t_en: How about I just toss a coin? Nah, that's not serious enough...

t_ch: 你又来啦…所以，愿意花些时间听听我的烦恼吗？
t_en: You're back... So, willing to spend some time to listen to my woes?

t_ch: 我…原本也算个小有名气的梦泡艺术家，代表作有《漆皮木偶与太空仓鼠》、《第十三个时刻》、《迷因的野餐》……
t_en: I... was a dream bubble artist of small renown, with my representative works being Patent Leather Puppet and Space Hamster, The Thirteenth Moment, and Meme's Picnic...

t_ch: 相信单从这些名字中你也能发现，我在艺术上的表达稍微有那么一点——好吧，对于一般人而言或许是过于——抽象和前沿。
t_en: I'm sure you've noticed from these names that my artistic expression is a little... All right, to most people, it might be overly... abstract and avant-garde.

t_ch: 我原本并不为此感到烦扰。曾经的我一直坚信「艺术」本该如此，天经地义。直到…我主动离开了鸢尾花家系。
t_en: I didn't think too much of that. I once firmly believed that art should be thus, until... I left the Iris Family of my own accord.

t_ch: 我的风格并未改变，口碑与名声却一落千丈…往日的赞美都变成了恶评，他们讥讽我、嘲笑我，将我的作品贬的一文不值。
t_en: My style didn't change, but my fame suffered greatly... Praises of the past became review bombs, and they ridiculed me, mocked me, and considered my art utterly worthless.

t_ch: 我现在有些…迷茫。明明我还是我，但没有了「鸢尾花」的光环之后，我似乎又不再是「我」了……
t_en: I am now somewhat... lost. I'm still me, but without the Iris Family's name, I don't seem to be "me" anymore...

t_ch: 那些鼓吹什么「酒香不怕巷子深的」，要么就是幸存者偏差，要么就是还没经过现实的毒打……
t_en: Those who drum on about "good wine needs no bush" either have experienced survivor bias or haven't been beaten up by the brutalities of reality yet...

t_ch: 我曾天真地认为，作为一名「艺术家」，我所需要做的就只是投身创作、尽情挥洒我的天赋与才华。
t_en: I once naively thought that, as an artist, all I need to do was to pour my creativity into my work and flaunt my artistic talent.

t_ch: 直到我从镶金的鸟笼子里钻了出来，才明白并不是只要作品够精彩、够独特，就一定能收获关注与喜爱。
t_en: Not until I wormed my way out of my gilded cage did I realize that a piece had to be more than just exciting and unique to receive attention and adoration.

t_ch: 当失去了为你打点好一切流程的的团队后，你得时刻关注市场动向和家族政策、得紧巴巴地控制资源投入与制作周期，你得分神打点资金人脉、亲自宣发造势，甚至还不得不费大力气处理盗版维权的问题……
t_en: When you lose a team that did everything for you, you have to pay attention to market trends and Family policies, stringently manage your resources and production timelines, personally promote yourself and manage your sponsors and network. You even have to spend huge amounts of effort on combating copyright disputes...

t_ch: 现在想来，过去的我是多么愚蠢…艺术的背后永远都少不了钱与权的影子，谁又能只靠一腔热情生存下去？
t_en: Now that I think of it, I was so dumb in the past... The shadows of finance and power are always pulling the strings behind the glamor of the art world. Who would be able to survive with just passion?

t_ch: 我曾天真地认为，作为一名「艺术家」，我所需要做的就只是投身创作、尽情挥洒我的天赋与才华。
t_en: I once naively thought that, as an artist, all I need to do was to pour my creativity into my work and flaunt my artistic talent.

t_ch: 直到我从镶金的鸟笼子里钻了出来，才明白并不是只要作品够精彩、够独特，就一定能收获关注与喜爱。
t_en: Not until I wormed my way out of my gilded cage did I realize that a piece had to be more than just exciting and unique to receive attention and adoration.

t_ch: 当失去了为你打点好一切流程的的团队后，你得时刻关注市场动向和家族政策、得紧巴巴地控制资源投入与制作周期，你得分神打点资金人脉、亲自宣发造势，甚至还不得不费大力气处理盗版维权的问题……
t_en: When you lose a team that did everything for you, you have to pay attention to market trends and Family policies, stringently manage your resources and production timelines, personally promote yourself and manage your sponsors and network. You even have to spend huge amounts of effort on combating copyright disputes...

t_ch: 现在想来，过去的我是多么愚蠢…艺术的背后永远都少不了钱与权的影子，谁又能只靠一腔热情生存下去？
t_en: Now that I think of it, I was so dumb in the past... The shadows of finance and power are always pulling the strings behind the glamor of the art world. Who would be able to survive with just passion?

t_ch: 说什么「只要努力就会有回报」…哈，这种鬼话也就傻子才会相信！
t_en: All that talk about how "hard work begets rewards"... Ha, only a fool would believe such lies!

t_ch: 那些「当红个人梦泡艺术家」，你以为他们的作品当真有多与众不同？算了吧，不过是刚巧赶上个好时机，顺势赚了一波流量罢了。
t_en: Those flavor-of-the-month indie dream bubble artists... you think their works are really one-of-a-kind? Perish the thought. They simply lucked out and rode the wave of popularity.

t_ch: 说实话，我现在如果想要重新回到鸢尾花家系，其实也没那么难。即便最近的作品口碑暴跌，我的影响力和粉丝基数始终还在，比再去培养一个同样风格的新人性价比高多了。
t_en: Honestly, it wouldn't be that hard if I really wanted to return to the Iris Family fold. Even if my recent works were lambasted, my influence and fan base are still intact. I'm still more cost-effective than building up a newbie with a similar style.

t_ch: 总的来说，现在的我对他们而言还有些价值…而且为艺人洗白、重新造势这种事情，他们向来擅长得很，根本花不了多大成本。
t_en: Anyway, I'm still of some value to them in my current state... They've always been excellent at resetting an artist's career and creating some new hype for them anyway. It doesn't cost that much to them.

t_ch: 虽然像是笼中鸟，但好歹吃喝不愁…这笔买卖怎么看都不亏吧，我在那一个劲儿纠结什么呢…哈、哈……
t_en: It's a cage, but at least I'll be clothed and fed... I see no downside to this deal, so why am I struggling with this decision... Ha, ha...

t_ch: 心有不甘又怎样、天赋异禀又如何——当钱和权成了高不可攀的门槛，所谓的艺术又怎么可能保持它的「纯粹」呢！
t_en: So what if I can't take it lying down? So what if I possess exceptional talent... When money and power become insurmountable barriers, how can the so-called "art" even preserve its purity!?

t_ch: 即便如此，我依旧心怀梦想……
t_en: Even so, I still have a dream...

t_ch: 男人痴痴地望着墙上的仓鼠球骑士海报，咧开的嘴角挂着餍足的笑意。
t_en: The man gazes, entranced, at the Hamster Ball Knight's poster on the wall. His lips are parted into a contented grin.

t_ch: 或许在他的幻想中，他已经克服了重重困难，获得了自由、实现了梦想…
t_en: Perhaps, in his fantasies, he has overcome the obstacles to become a free man and achieved his dream...

t_ch: 你决定悄悄走开，不去惊扰这场梦中的白日梦。
t_en: You walk away quietly so as not to disturb his daydream inside the dream.

t_ch: 真是…令人羡慕啊……
t_en: I'm green with envy...

t_ch: 眼前的皮皮西正仰头望着半空中的哈努兄弟立牌，面色怅然。他身上有种独特的气质，在这熙攘喧闹的人群中显得格格不入。
t_en: The Pepeshi before you is looking up at the Hanu sign in midair with a sorrowful face. He has a unique aura that makes him stand out in the cacophonous crowd.

t_ch: 你思索半晌，突然意识到，那是「孤独」。
t_en: After contemplating for a while, it suddenly occurs to you that this aura is "loneliness."

t_ch: 羡慕什么？
t_en: What are you envious of?

t_ch: …没什么，我只是有些羡慕…钟表小子和哈努兄弟之间的友情罢了。
t_en: ...Nothing much. I'm just a little envious of the... friendship that Clockie and Hanu share.

t_ch: 虽然我并没怎么看过钟表小子的动画，却依旧为他们之间纯粹的友谊而动容。
t_en: Although I've never watched much of Clockie's animation, I'm still moved by the pure friendship between them.

t_ch: 你是哈努兄弟的粉丝？
t_en: Are you a fan of Hanu?

t_ch: 哈哈，并不是，我甚至没怎么看过动画。但不得不说，他和钟表小子之间纯粹的友谊令我非常羡慕。
t_en: Haha, not really. I actually barely watched the animations. But still, I find the pure friendship between Clockie and him very enviable.

t_ch: 我也有一位…很重要的朋友。但我们之间的关系出现了一些…裂痕。实不相瞒，我正深受此事困扰。
t_en: I, too, have an... important friend, but our friendship is going through... a rough patch. In fact, I'm quite upset because of this.

t_ch: 为了向他证明自己，我来到了匹诺康尼，成为一名梦境鼓励师。只可惜…时至今日我依旧没能找到合适的方式来解决我们之间的问题。或许我们之间的友谊…已经走到了尽头。
t_en: To prove myself to him, I ventured to Penacony and became a Dreamscape Motivator. Unfortunately... I still have yet to come up with any solutions that can help us mend fences to this day. Perhaps... our friendship is already over.

t_ch: 啊…抱歉，自顾自说了这么多，希望没影响到你的心情。祝你玩得开心！
t_en: Oh, forgive me for rambling on. I hope your mood isn't affected. Have a great time!

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: …和我吗？如果你想的话，当然没什么问题。
t_en: ...With me? If that's what you want, be my guest.

t_ch: 聊聊你的朋友？
t_en: Would you like to talk about your friend?

t_ch: 嗯…这么说或许有些扫兴，但抱歉，我现在还没准备好谈论关于他的话题。
t_en: Well... I'm sorry for disappointing you, but I'm not ready to talk about him at the moment.

t_ch: 如果你不介意的话，我们可以聊一些别的？
t_en: How about we talk about something else if you don't mind?

t_ch: 我们是不是见过。
t_en: Haven't we met before?

t_ch: 这么一说…哦，我想起来了…你是那时候拜托安娜帮忙的年轻人吧？
t_en: Oh, I remember you. You were the youngster who asked for Anna's help back then, right?

t_ch: 听说后来事情被妥善解决了，想来一定有你不少功劳吧。
t_en: I heard that the matter was successfully resolved in the end. You must have played a big role in it.

t_ch: 真是有活力啊……
t_en: How energetic...

t_ch: 不聊了。
t_en: That's it for today.

t_ch: 嗯，那么，祝你玩得开心。
t_en: Well, I hope you have a wonderful time.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 你…？如果你是在开玩笑，很抱歉我现在并没有这个心情；但如果你是认真的，请立刻打消这个念头——否则我会如实向猎犬反馈！
t_en: What...? If you're joking, I'm sorry. I'm not in the mood for that right now. If you're serious about it, I'd suggest that you drop the idea immediately, or I'll report you to the Bloodhounds!

t_ch: 那么现在，请离我远点。
t_en: Now, please stay away from me.

t_ch: 我先走了，拜拜。
t_en: I've got to go. Bye.

t_ch: 回见，如果你之后需要梦境鼓励师的帮助，倒是不妨来找我聊聊。
t_en: See you around. Feel free to come to me if you ever need the help of a Dreamscape Motivator.

t_ch: 眼前的皮皮西依旧仰头望着半空中哈努兄弟的立牌，面色怅然。
t_en: The Pepeshi before you is still looking up at the Hanu sign in midair with a sorrowful face.

t_ch: 动画中的友谊总是那么纯粹，那么美好。哈努兄弟永远是钟表小子的好伙伴，没有争吵、没有背叛、没有…算了，没什么。
t_en: The friendships portrayed in animations are always so pure and beautiful. Hanu is always a good companion of Clockie's. They don't fight, betray each other, or... forget it.

t_ch: 无论如何，我和那个男人的孽缘…也该有个了结了。
t_en: Anyway, it's probably time for the karma between me and that guy... to come to an end.

t_ch: 哈、哈啊…是啊…我究竟还要自欺欺人到什么时候呢……
t_en: Ha... Haha... Yeah... When am I going to stop fooling myself?

t_ch: 我的那位老朋友…他曾是我的向导、我的领路人，可最后…他反倒成了我前行的最大阻碍。我为此感到…遗憾。
t_en: That old friend of mine... He used to be my role model and mentor, but... he ended up becoming the biggest obstacle to my progress. I feel... regretful about that.

t_ch: 我们有过很多很多次争吵。起初他谴责、而我试图辩解，后来我们互不相让，再后来我们之间只剩数不尽的、令人难堪的谩骂。
t_en: We've argued many, many times. When he condemned me at first, I tried to defend myself. As time went on, neither of us gave in. We just kept hurling insults at each other in the end.

t_ch: 他开始一本又一本地著书立说，驳斥一切在他看来的异端邪说；而我则只身来到匹诺康尼，成为了一名梦境鼓励师……
t_en: He began penning book after book to refute everything he deemed heretical, while I traveled to Penacony all by myself to become a Dreamscape Motivator...

t_ch: 哈，话虽这么说，其实我的工作性质和「筑梦师」更类似——作为对自身理论的实践，我帮家族设计制作了一些小玩意…比如大街上那些窥梦电话。
t_en: Having said that, the nature of my work is actually similar to the one of a Dreamweaver. As practical applications of my theories, I've designed some gadgets for The Family... like the Dreampeek Call on the streets.

t_ch: 我们都自顾自地沉浸在自己的领域里，自顾自地想要证明自己才是正确的那一个。只不过这一切如今早已没有了意义……
t_en: We were both obsessed with our fields, trying hard to prove that we were the right one. However, this fight had long lost its meaning...

t_ch: 到了最后…就连争吵的声音也消失了。我们之间仅剩沉默。
t_en: At the end of the day... we didn't even argue with each other anymore. What was left between us was sheer silence.

t_ch: 我承认自己是个懦夫，但我无论如何也无法接受…他居然先我而去了，从此我们再也没有和好的可能……
t_en: I know I'm a coward, yet there's no way I can ever accept the fact that... he passed away before me. There's no chance for us to ever patch things up...

t_ch: …或许，我该向家族寻求一些帮助……
t_en: ...Perhaps, I should seek help from The Family...

t_ch: 我的那位老朋友…他曾是我的向导、我的领路人，可最后…他反倒成了我前行的最大阻碍。我为此感到…遗憾。
t_en: That old friend of mine... He used to be my role model and mentor, but... he ended up becoming the biggest obstacle to my progress. I feel... regretful about that.

t_en: We've argued many, many times. When he condemned me at first, I tried to defend myself. As time went on, neither of us gave in. We just kept hurling insults at each other in the end.

t_en: He began penning book after book to refute everything he deemed heretical, while I traveled to Penacony all by myself to become a Dreamscape Motivator...

t_en: We were both obsessed with our fields, trying hard to prove that we were the right one. However, this fight had long lost its meaning...

t_en: At the end of the day... we didn't even argue with each other anymore. What was left between us was sheer silence.

t_ch: 我承认自己是个懦夫，但我无论如何也无法接受…他居然先我而去了，从此我们再也没有和好的可能……
t_en: I know I'm a coward, yet there's no way I can ever accept the fact that... he passed away before me. There's no chance for us to ever patch things up...

t_ch: …或许，我该向家族寻求一些帮助……
t_en: ...Perhaps, I should seek help from The Family...

t_ch: 他有什么资格说我背叛了他！哈哈，他自己又做了些什么？
t_en: How dare he accuse me of betraying him!? Haha, what about his own doings?

t_ch: 一个博识学会的学者，竟然想要垄断知识——这种事情传出去，只会是天大的笑话！
t_en: As a scholar of the Intelligentsia Guild, he actually thought of monopolizing knowledge. What a massive joke that would be if others heard about it!

t_ch: 我承认他在「梦与心灵」领域的研究无人可及，但这不意味着他能成为一个独裁者、能掌握对思想和话语的生杀大权！
t_en: Indeed, his research in the field of dreams and psychology was unparalleled, but that didn't give him the right to be a tyrant! Or to decide whether some thoughts and speech should exist!

t_ch: 看看他都做了什么——通过故弄玄虚的仪式、神秘晦涩的行话，将普通的学术交流变成门徒选拔大会…人们不再拥有自由交流思想的权利，所有反对他的声音都将成为异端邪说。
t_en: Look at what he did — he turned ordinary academic conferences into ceremonies for him to pick his disciples by setting up contrived rituals and throwing in convoluted jargon... People were no longer free to speak their minds and converse with each other, as all voices opposing him were labeled as heretical.

t_ch: 他甚至…博识尊在上…甚至妄图将我变为刽子手与帮凶！
t_en: He even... oh, by Nous... attempted to make me an executioner and his accomplice!

t_ch: 而我只是想按自己的方式去追求知识、去构建自己的学说，就被斥为「背叛」…荒谬、荒谬！
t_en: And I was deemed a traitor for wanting to pursue knowledge and form my doctrine in my own way... How ridiculous!

t_ch: 他究竟为什么…会变成这副样子呢？
t_en: Just why... did he turn out this way?

t_ch: 一个博识学会的学者，竟然想要垄断知识——这种事情传出去，只会是天大的笑话！
t_en: As a scholar of the Intelligentsia Guild, he actually thought of monopolizing knowledge. What a massive joke that would be if others heard about it!

t_en: Look at what he did — he turned ordinary academic conferences into ceremonies for him to pick his disciples by setting up contrived rituals and throwing in convoluted jargon... People were no longer free to speak their minds and converse with each other, as all voices opposing him were labeled as heretical.

t_ch: 他甚至…博识尊在上…甚至妄图将我变为刽子手与帮凶！
t_en: He even... oh, by Nous... attempted to make me an executioner and his accomplice!

t_ch: 而我只是想按自己的方式去追求知识、去构建自己的学说，就被斥为「背叛」…荒谬、荒谬！
t_en: And I was deemed a traitor for wanting to pursue knowledge and form my doctrine in my own way... How ridiculous!

t_ch: 他究竟为什么…会变成这副样子呢？
t_en: Just why... did he turn out this way?

t_ch: 皮皮西的嘴角浮现起一抹微笑，这使得他看上去更加年轻、也更有活力了一些。
t_en: The Pepeshi breaks into a faint grin that makes him appear more youthful and vibrant.

t_ch: 他的目光落在虚空某处，眼中浮现出一种满溢怀念的欣喜。
t_en: When he sets his gaze on a spot in the empty air, there is a hint of nostalgic delight in his eyes.

t_ch: 你知道吗？第一次见面的时候，我们明明相约共进晚餐，却未曾想一直聊到了第二天的早饭。
t_en: Did you know that, the first time we met, our dinner lasted all through the night till breakfast the following day? We had so much to talk about.

t_ch: 现在我还清晰地记得那天早上的菜单，就像我还记得与他聊过的每一个话题。
t_en: I can still remember the breakfast menu that morning, as clearly as I can remember every conversation I've had with him.

t_ch: 那时候我是如此地崇拜着他——比起朋友，他更像是我的父亲，我的导师。现在他已经走了，这些话也没有那么难以启齿了…是，我的确曾经崇拜着他，依赖着他。
t_en: I looked up to him so much at that time. He was more like a father and a mentor to me. Now that he is gone, I can say these things a lot more easily... Yes, I once idolized and relied on him.

t_ch: 我曾经最大的梦想就是为他搭建一座专属的学术舞台，然后看他如何在那台上绽放光芒、折服众生……
t_en: Once upon a time, my greatest aspiration was to create an academic platform only for him, where he could shine and impress everyone with his wisdom...

t_ch: 他是如此的无可替代…假如这梦境能让我永远沉浸在美好的回忆里，也好。
t_en: He was irreplaceable... It would be great if I could forever immerse myself in those beautiful memories.

t_ch: 抱歉，我不喜欢在上班时间闲聊。
t_en: Sorry, but I don't like to chat during working hours.

t_ch: 介意聊会天吗？
t_en: Mind if we have a little chat?

t_ch: 既然你有兴趣，我们就来聊聊吧。
t_en: Since you're up for it, let's do it.

t_ch: 你的工作感觉如何？
t_en: How do you feel about your job?

t_ch: 这里的工作？说实话，不算刺激。
t_en: My job here? Frankly speaking, it isn't the most interesting thing.

t_ch: 麦考先生对公馆的安保要求十分严格，而我负责将他的命令传达给猎犬家系的小伙子们。
t_en: Mr. McCoy is stringent about the security of Dewlight Pavilion. My job is to convey Mr. McCoy's instructions to the Bloodhound guys.

t_ch: 直到现在，还没有敌人能入侵这里。所以我们每天也就是做些惯例的巡逻，偶尔帮着招待一下客人什么的…平淡的日常啊。
t_en: No one has ever succeeded in breaking into this place, so we just patrol the place every day and occasionally assist in entertaining the guests... That's a pretty uneventful routine.

t_ch: 不过，这正是美梦安宁的象征，不是吗？
t_en: Still, it means things are peaceful in the Dreamscape, isn't it?

t_ch: 你如何看待美梦？
t_en: What are your thoughts on dreams?

t_ch: 哦，美梦，任何家人都不会否认它存在的必要。不过它并不是我们生活的全部。
t_en: Oh, dreams. No one in The Family would deny its importance, but it isn't the only thing that matters in my life.

t_ch: 就拿我来说吧，除了工作，其余时间我都会回到现实，陪伴家人和宠物们——我家里养了几十只小动物。
t_en: Just take me as an example. When I'm not working, I'll return to reality to spend time with my family and pets — I have dozens of animals at home.

t_ch: 这么多？！
t_en: Dozens!?

t_ch: 嗯…基本都是我收养的流浪动物。最开始只有两三只，时间一长就多起来了……
t_en: Yup... They were stray animals that I took in. I only had a couple of them in the beginning, and got more as time went on...

t_ch: 你应该认识下托帕……
t_en: You should meet Miss Topaz...

t_ch: 虽然不知道那是谁，不过我猜她一定也是个爱宠人士，哈哈。
t_en: I have no idea who that is, but I bet she must be a pet lover too, haha.

t_ch: 我家里基本都是收养的流浪动物。最开始只有两三只，时间一长就多起来了……
t_en: Most of my pets used to be stray animals. I only had a couple of them in the beginning, and they got more as time went on...

t_ch: 对于这一做法，我从没感觉过后悔或者嫌麻烦。毕竟，现实里的问题也总得有人解决，不是吗？
t_en: I've never regretted doing this or found it a nuisance. After all, someone has to deal with the issues in the real world at the end of the day, right?

t_ch: 你怎么看待美梦剧团？
t_en: What's your take on the Sweet Dreams Troupe?

t_ch: 噢，美梦剧团，可爱又乖巧的小东西，我非常喜欢。
t_en: Oh, Sweet Dreams Troupe. They are a lovely and sweet bunch. I like them.

t_ch: 作为梦境的造物，它们在服务宾客方面丝毫不逊色于我们人类，是很可靠的小帮手。
t_en: Although they are Dreamscape creations, they're just as good as us humans when it comes to serving the guests. They're quite reliable assistants.

t_ch: 不幸的是，在服务宾客的过程中难免会有情绪堆积。一些美梦剧团因此被侵蚀成了…不那么美妙的样子。
t_en: Unfortunately, it's inevitable to get frustrated while handling the guests. It's unfortunate that some Sweet Dreams Troupes are corroded as a result and are reduced to... something less sweet.

t_ch: 倘若有一天，梦里的人们能不再有负面情绪，剧团或许也能始终保持纯粹——那会是真正的美梦。
t_en: If one day people in the Dreamscape could be rid of their negative emotions, the troupe could probably stay pure all the time — that would be a beautiful dream in the truest sense.

t_ch: 就先聊到这儿吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 再见，下次再聊。
t_en: Catch you later.

t_ch: 再见，祝你度过愉快的一天。
t_en: See you, and I hope you have a wonderful day.

t_ch: 里奥的表情冷若冰霜。他没有说话，也没人敢去搭话。
t_en: Leo wears a frosty expression. He isn't uttering a word, and no one dares to talk to him.

t_ch: …失礼了，我很少表现得像刚才那般失态。
t_en: ...My apologies. That behavior was out of line. I am not usually like that.

t_ch: 我不喜欢在旁人面前表现情绪，那会为我的工作带来困扰。这次…是意外。我保证不会有下次了。
t_en: I do not like to reveal my emotions in public as it will affect my work. This time... this is an accident. It won't happen again.

t_ch: 美梦终有一天会迎来幻灭……
t_en: Dreams will one day face their end...

t_ch: 美梦…唉，美梦中本不该存在悲伤，但有时我就是抑制不住悲观的心态。
t_en: Dreams... One shouldn't feel sad in a dream, but I just can't help feeling pessimistic sometimes.

t_ch: 是，我们当然需要美梦，「黄金的时刻」是一座梦之城。正因心存对美好的渴望，人们才会来到这个地方。
t_en: There's no doubt we need dreams. The Golden Hour is a city of dreams where people who desire beauty visit.

t_ch: 但梦的扩张已经逼近极限，所有能享的福都终有穷尽。如今的匹诺康尼就像一个肥皂泡，再想变大就只能迎来破碎。
t_en: Nevertheless, the expansion of dreams is approaching its limits, and all the nice things will eventually come to exhaustion. Penacony is akin to a bubble that is bound to burst if it continues to grow bigger.

t_ch: 或许在未来的某天，全宇宙都会厌倦梦境，连带着厌倦一切幻想…到了那时，我们这些人又该能做些什么呢？
t_en: There might come a day when the entire universe gets sick of the Dreamscape as well as the illusions that come with it... When the time comes, what can people like us do?

t_ch: 唯一值得庆幸的是，我们暂时还看不到那么一天。
t_en: The only good thing is that such a day won't be happening in the near future.

t_ch: 在这朝露公馆以外，尽是谎言与欺骗！
t_en: The world beyond Dewlight Pavilion is full of lies and deception!

t_ch: 嘶…家族之外，危机四伏啊！
t_en: Hmm... Dangers are lurking everywhere outside The Family!

t_ch: 有怪物入侵梦境了？
t_en: Has the Dreamscape been invaded by monsters?

t_ch: 公司的人打过来了？
t_en: Are the IPC here to attack us?

t_ch: 不，我只是想起了一件糗事。总有些逐梦客让人气到牙痒痒！
t_en: No, I just recalled something embarrassing that happened to me. There are always some Dreamchaser who never fail to get under my skin!

t_ch: 有次我离开公馆去「黄金的时刻」采购，在路边遇到了一个带货的逐梦客。
t_en: There was a time I went out to buy something in the Golden Hour. I bumped into a Dreamchaser who worked as a salesman along the way.

t_ch: 他说自己手上有来自朋克洛德的「以太卡带」，急着出手，只要五万信用点就卖给我。当时我只带了苜蓿钞，跟家人们借了个遍才凑齐那么多信用点。
t_en: He claimed to own an Aether Cartridge from Punklorde and was eager to sell it to me for just <unbreak>50,000</unbreak> credits. I only brought Alfalfa credits with me at that time, so I had to borrow from some of my family to afford it.

t_ch: 结果后来，我在星际网络上搜到了一模一样的同款，就是一盘普通的磁带——才五百信用点不到！
t_en: In the end, I came across another cartridge that looked exactly the same on the Interastral network as the one I bought. It was just a regular cartridge worth less than <unbreak>500</unbreak> credits!

t_ch: 真是大坑啊！难道人与人之间连最基本的信任都没有了？！愿神主教训这些破坏「同谐」的商人！
t_en: That was an outright scam! Can people nowadays still be trusted!? May the Great One punish the merchants who disrupt Harmony!

t_ch: 更可气的是，家族还不能对他做出过分的惩罚，只能将他逐出梦境…我不甘心啊啊啊啊！
t_en: Much to my dismay, The Family only ousted the guy from the Dreamscape without any heavy sentences... That's unacceptable!!!

t_ch: 嘶…某些逐梦客，真是让人气到牙痒痒！
t_en: Hmph... Some Dreamchasers are just so annoying!

t_ch: 有次我离开公馆去「黄金的时刻」采购，在路边遇到了一个带货的逐梦客。
t_en: There was a time I went out to buy something in the Golden Hour. I bumped into a Dreamchaser who worked as a salesman along the way.

t_ch: 他说自己手上有来自朋克洛德的「以太卡带」，急着出手，只要五万信用点就卖给我。当时我只带了苜蓿钞，跟家人们借了个遍才凑齐那么多信用点。
t_en: He claimed to own an Aether Cartridge from Punklorde and was eager to sell it to me for just <unbreak>50,000</unbreak> credits. I only brought Alfalfa credits with me at that time, so I had to borrow from some of my family to afford it.

t_ch: 结果后来，我在星际网络上搜到了一模一样的同款，就是一盘普通的磁带——才五百信用点不到！
t_en: In the end, I came across another cartridge that looked exactly the same on the Interastral network as the one I bought. It was just a regular cartridge worth less than <unbreak>500</unbreak> credits!

t_ch: 真是大坑啊！难道人与人之间连最基本的信任都没有了？！愿神主教训这些破坏「同谐」的商人！
t_en: That was an outright scam! Can people nowadays still be trusted!? May the Great One punish the merchants who disrupt Harmony!

t_ch: 更可气的是，家族还不能对他做出过分的惩罚，只能将他逐出梦境…我不甘心啊啊啊啊！
t_en: Much to my dismay, The Family only ousted the guy from the Dreamscape without any heavy sentences... That's unacceptable!!!

t_ch: 你好啊。如果需要找人倾诉的话，我愿意聆听。
t_en: Hello there. If you have anything to get off your chest, I'm all ears.

t_ch: 数种情绪在短短一瞬间划过里奥的脸颊。最终，他仿佛大梦初醒般露出了一抹微笑。
t_en: Several different emotions flicker across Leo's face in a short time. In the end, he breaks into a faint smile as if he's just woken up from a dream.

t_ch: 哦，天呐，天呐…我都错过了些什么！
t_en: Oh, dear... What have I missed?

t_ch: 我拥有的这份工作是多么美妙啊——不用东跑西颠，不会感到劳累，下班后还能回家照看我的小狗。
t_en: It just dawned on me how perfect my job actually was — I don't have to run errands at different places. It's not too tiring, and I still have time to take care of my dogs after work.

t_ch: 仔细想想，之前的我真是太死板了。这样惬意的日子，就该笑着过才对！
t_en: Come to think of it, I've been too rigid all this while. I should be enjoying such a wonderful life with a smile!

t_ch: 或许是时候改掉这副冷脸了…从现在起，我要热情接纳身边的每一个人——就从你开始吧。
t_en: Perhaps, it's time I get rid of this poker face... From now on, I must treat everyone around me with warmth. Let's start with you.

t_ch: 请你收下这份情绪作为礼物，我将它珍藏了很久，很久。
t_en: This emotion is a gift for you. I've kept it for a very long time...

t_ch: 数种情绪在短短一瞬间划过里奥的脸颊。最终，他仿佛大梦初醒般露出了一抹微笑。
t_en: Several different emotions flicker across Leo's face in a short time. In the end, he breaks into a faint smile as if he's just woken up from a dream.

t_ch: 哦，天呐，天呐…我都错过了些什么！
t_en: Oh, dear... What have I missed?

t_ch: 我拥有的这份工作是多么美妙啊——不用东跑西颠，不会感到劳累，下班后还能回家照看我的小狗。
t_en: It just dawned on me how perfect my job actually was — I don't have to run errands at different places. It's not too tiring, and I still have time to take care of my dogs after work.

t_ch: 仔细想想，之前的我真是太死板了。这样惬意的日子，就该笑着过才对！
t_en: Come to think of it, I've been too rigid all this while. I should be enjoying such a wonderful life with a smile!

t_ch: 或许是时候改掉这副冷脸了…从现在起，我要热情接纳身边的每一个人——就从你开始吧。
t_en: Perhaps, it's time I get rid of this poker face... From now on, I must treat everyone around me with warmth. Let's start with you.

t_ch: …你看起来像个新面孔，想见识一下我的大宝贝吗？
t_en: ...You seem to be new in town. Would you like to take a look at this great item that I own?

t_ch: 什么宝贝？
t_en: What's that?

t_ch: 你是商人？
t_en: Are you a merchant?

t_ch: 你别乱来！
t_en: Don't mess with me!

t_ch: 哦对，你应该还不知道我「机动骑士」亨德列克的名号……
t_en: Right, I think you have never heard of me. I'm Hendrick the Mobile Knight.

t_ch: 自我介绍下，我是家族中最为幸运和强大的皮皮西——我拥有神主赐予的高科技战斗机甲！
t_en: Let me tell you something about myself — I'm the luckiest and strongest Pepeshi in The Family. I have a high-tech combat mech bestowed by the Great One!

t_ch: 看这右肩上的漆黑炮口，左臂上的伸缩能量刀，还有背后的双缸推进器…就知道这身战甲是极品啦！
t_en: Look at the black cannon on its right shoulder, the retractable energy blade on its left arm, and the dual thrusters on its back... I bet you can tell it is an elite armor!

t_ch: 可是你身上什么都没有…
t_en: But I can't see you wearing anything...

t_ch: 对哦，只有{RUBY_B#「家族」成员}「家人」{RUBY_E#}才能目视它的光辉，像你这样的外宾什么也看不到…真可惜。
t_en: I almost forgot about this — only {RUBY_B#members of The Family}"kinsmen"{RUBY_E#} can see its glow. It's a shame that it isn't visible to an outsider like you.

t_ch: 我怎么看不见？
t_en: Why can't I see anything?

t_ch: 对哦，只有{RUBY_B#「家族」成员}「家人」{RUBY_E#}才能目视它的光辉，像你这样的外宾什么也看不到…真可惜。
t_en: I almost forgot about this — only {RUBY_B#members of The Family}"kinsmen"{RUBY_E#} can see its glow. It's a shame that it isn't visible to an outsider like you.

t_ch: 哇哦，太酷了！
t_en: Wow, it's so cool!

t_ch: 哈哈，我就说吧！
t_en: Told you!

t_ch: 等等…这不对劲！按理来说，应该只有{RUBY_B#「家族」成员}「家人」{RUBY_E#}才能目视它的光辉才对——你明明什么都看不到，干嘛装出一幅很兴奋的样子！
t_en: Wait, this isn't right! Normally, only {RUBY_B#members of The Family}"kinsmen"{RUBY_E#} can see its glow. Why did you fake your excitement when you couldn't see anything?

t_ch: 我想跟你聊聊。
t_en: Can we talk...?

t_ch: 哈，你也对我的战甲感兴趣了？
t_en: Ha, you're intrigued by my armor, aren't you?

t_ch: 给我介绍一下你的战甲。
t_en: Tell me more about your armor.

t_ch: 我这身战甲名叫「全领域歼击动力铠」，放眼整个宇宙也是数一数二的上品。
t_en: It's called "All-area annihilating power armor," one of the best of its kind in the entire universe.

t_ch: 穿上它之后，哪怕虫群和军团携手入侵朝露公馆，也会被我打得落花流水！
t_en: Even if the Swarm and the Legion were to launch a joint attack on Dewlight Pavilion now, I would be able to crush them when wearing it!

t_ch: 它们应该进不来梦境吧…
t_en: I don't think those two can come into the Dreamscape...

t_ch: 确定不是靠摇人打赢？
t_en: Are you sure you wouldn't win due to reinforcements?

t_ch: 咳咳，总之就是很厉害！你只需要接受这个设定就好。
t_en: Ahem. The armor is awesome. That's all I'm saying! You just need to accept that setting.

t_ch: 不过战斗并不是战甲唯一的功能——我经常开着它带家人们出去兜风，大伙都赞不绝口呢。
t_en: Combat isn't the only function of the armor though. I often take my kin out for a ride in it. Everyone loves it.

t_ch: 你是怎么得到战甲的？
t_en: How did you get the armor?

t_ch: 说来话长，从我还是个孩子时起，这套战甲就已然浮现于我身……
t_en: It's a long story. This mecha had manifested on my body since I was little...

t_ch: 那是一个平静的午后。妈妈在厨房做饭，我在沙发上玩着模型小人。安逸与香味一同在空气中弥散。
t_en: It was a peaceful afternoon. Mom was cooking in the kitchen, while I was playing with some figurines on the couch. Tranquility was in the air along with the mouthwatering aroma of food.

t_ch: 可空气中很快就多出了另一股味道——刺鼻的浓烟。
t_en: But soon, there came a pungent smell of smoke.

t_ch: 突如其来的光芒笼罩了一切。火焰冲上天空，将这副美景烧成灰烬。
t_en: Suddenly, everything was enveloped by a glaring light. Flames shot up toward the sky, turning this beautiful scene into ashes.

t_ch: 我不顾一切地爬向厨房，抱起受伤的妈妈，飞奔到屋子外面。热浪没能烧灼我的皮肤，废墟也没能阻拦我的脚步……
t_en: Without hesitation, I crawled my way to the kitchen, carried my injured mother, and ran outside the house. The heat did not burn my skin, and the debris did not impede my way.

t_ch: 哪怕以皮皮西的尺度来看，一个孩子也不可能做到这些…这定是神主的恩赐！
t_en: Even by the Pepeshi's standard, a child couldn't have done all that... It must have been a gift from the Great One!

t_ch: 我平日乐于助人，偶尔做点无害的幻想。神主认为我是好的，便向我投来注视。这是良善的回报。
t_en: I enjoyed helping others and occasionally daydreamed harmlessly. The Great One must have thought I was good and decided to cast THEIR gaze upon me. It was the reward for my kindness.

t_ch: 直到现在，那副战甲依然披挂于我身。
t_en: I'm still wearing the armor now.

t_ch: 你如何看待美梦？
t_en: What are your thoughts on dreams?

t_ch: 你是在暗戳戳讽刺我的战甲只是做梦吗？开玩笑的~
t_en: Are you suggesting that the armor only exists in my dream? Don't worry. I'm just messing with you.

t_ch: 我从来没有怀疑过家族事业的正确性！不过现在的美梦…确实有些糟乱。
t_en: I've never doubted whether what The Family is doing is correct! However, I must say the Dreamscape is indeed a bit chaotic now.

t_ch: 随着入梦的人数越来越多，各种胡作非为的闹剧也开始层出不穷。
t_en: As more and more people are flocking to the dream, cases of unruly behavior have been on the rise.

t_ch: 出于「神主」的仁慈，家族只会对这样的行为作出制止和口头教育，不会实行过分的惩罚。
t_en: Out of the benevolence of the Great One, The Family only addresses such behavior through intervention and verbal warnings without giving out severe sanctions.

t_ch: 但总有些逐梦客不识好歹、不长记性，光我自己就曾经三次抓到过同一个破坏公物的家伙！
t_en: Yet, some Dreamchasers just never learn their lessons. I've caught the same guy damaging public property three times myself!

t_ch: 唉，希望这种情况有朝一日能有所改善吧。
t_en: Alas, I hope things will get better one day.

t_ch: 先不聊了。
t_en: Talk to you later.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 喂喂，我的战甲可是装有反击电弧的，当心被烫掉手指头！
t_en: Hey, my armor comes with a counterattacking electric current. Be careful, or your fingers might be burned off!

t_ch: 下次再见。
t_en: See you around.

t_ch: 再见。希望有朝一日，你也能欣赏到我的战甲。
t_en: Goodbye. I look forward to the day when you can see my armor.

t_ch: 哟，老兄，来见识下我的战甲吗？
t_en: Hey buddy, wanna see my armor?

t_ch: …没想到作为「家人」，我居然也会表现出如此强烈的情绪，真够失态的。
t_en: ...I didn't know I could get so emotional as a "kinsman." That was so shameful.

t_ch: 难道是战甲的镇静模块失效了？不应该啊…之后去补充点冷却液吧。
t_en: Has the calming module of my armor stopped working? It shouldn't be... Let me replenish the coolant later.

t_ch: 能源…战甲缺少能源……
t_en: Energy... The armor needs energy...

t_ch: 皮皮西头顶的绒球突然蔫了下来，仿佛一根枯草般搭在脑壳边缘。
t_en: The fluff ball on the Pepeshi's head suddenly droops down and rests on the edge of his head like a withered weed.

t_ch: 唉，生亦何欢，死亦何苦…即便拥有如此强大的战甲，许多事依旧无能为力……
t_en: Alas, life doesn't guarantee pleasure, and death isn't all about pain... Even with such powerful armor, many things are still beyond my control.

t_ch: 不对，好像突然有一股奇怪的情感涌上心头。按理说不该发生这种事的……
t_en: Hold on. I can feel a weird sentiment getting stronger. This isn't supposed to happen...

t_ch: …可能是战甲缺少能源？是时候狂吃飞碟堡补充快乐元素了。
t_en: ...Maybe the armor needs more energy? It's time to chow down on some Floatdisc Burgers for more of that happy element.

t_ch: 这让我回想起去年的那场邂逅…不行，现在不是说这个的时候！
t_en: It reminds me of the encounter with a certain someone last year... No, now isn't the time to discuss that!

t_ch: 必须抑制这危险的情感。要忍耐…对，忍耐……
t_en: I have to suppress this dangerous emotion. I need to be patient... pa... tient...

t_ch: 皮皮西头顶的绒球突然蔫了下来，仿佛一根枯草般搭在脑壳边缘。
t_en: The fluff ball on the Pepeshi's head suddenly droops down and rests on the edge of his head like a withered weed.

t_ch: 唔…突然有一股奇怪的情感涌上心头。按理说不该发生这种事的……
t_en: Hmm... I can feel a weird sentiment getting stronger. This isn't supposed to happen...

t_ch: …可能是战甲缺少能源？是时候狂吃飞碟堡补充快乐元素了。
t_en: ...Maybe the armor needs more energy? It's time to chow down on some Floatdisc Burgers for more of that happy element.

t_ch: 这让我回想起去年的那场邂逅…不行，现在不是说这个的时候！
t_en: It reminds me of the encounter with a certain someone last year... No, now isn't the time to discuss that!

t_ch: 必须抑制这危险的情感。要忍耐…对，忍耐……
t_en: I have to suppress this dangerous emotion. I need to be patient... pa... tient...

t_ch: 歼灭模式，启动！
t_en: Annihilation mode activated!

t_ch: 皮皮西的脸色突然开始变红，仿佛一块逐渐加热的烙铁——这可真是太神奇啦。
t_en: The Pepeshi's cheeks suddenly turn red like an iron block heading up — what a miraculous sight to behold.

t_ch: 是动力炉短路了吗？我感觉战甲内的温度正在上升……
t_en: Is my power furnace short-circuited? I can feel the temperature within my armor rising...

t_ch: 我明白了，是我的热血在燃烧！一想到梦境里的种种乱象，我的炮口就开始蠢蠢欲动！
t_en: I've got it! The heat comes from my burning enthusiasm! I feel the urge to fire my cannon every time I think of the chaotic situation in the dream!

t_ch: 既然家族不愿用强硬的手段惩罚坏蛋，就由我来代为教训他们好了！
t_en: Since The Family doesn't want to be harsh on the bad guys, I'll do the job!

t_ch: 我是火焰，是黑暗骑士，是一切邪恶的终结者！
t_en: I shall be the blaze and the dark knight that puts all evil to an end!

t_ch: 我要开启歼灭模式，把那些扰乱梦境治安的家伙通通<size=28>轰~杀~成~渣</size>呀！
t_en: I'll activate the annihilation mode to <size=28>shred</size> into pieces those who disrupt the law and order in the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 哦哦哦，我的战甲正在以<unbreak>12</unbreak>倍功率运转！
t_en: Wow, my armor is overclocking <unbreak>12</unbreak> times more than its original rate!

t_ch: 皮皮西头顶的绒球开始不停摆动，仿佛飞行器的螺旋桨——要是速度再快一点，说不定他真能飞起来。
t_en: The fluff ball on the Pepeshi's head starts swinging vigorously like the propeller of an aircraft. He might be able to fly if it spins a little bit faster.

t_ch: 哦…哦！这是什么？我的战甲正在以<unbreak>12</unbreak>倍功率运转，感觉好像要飞起来了！
t_en: Oh... Oh? What's happening? My armor is overlocking <unbreak>12</unbreak> times more than its original rate! It feels like I'm going to fly!

t_ch: 是因为开启了推进系统的缘故吗…不对，是因为我好嗨！
t_en: Is it because I've turned on the propulsion system... No, it's because I'm excited!

t_ch: 干脆就这样冲上云霄吧，突破天际！
t_en: Why don't I just soar through the clouds and traverse across the sky like this!

t_ch: 一股粉色的气息从他身上涌出，弥散到周遭的空气中。那是肉眼可见的热情。
t_en: He radiates a pink glow that permeates the air around him — it visibly represents his enthusiasm.

t_ch: 皮皮西头顶的绒球开始不停摆动，仿佛飞行器的螺旋桨——要是速度再快一点，说不定他真能飞起来。
t_en: The fluff ball on the Pepeshi's head starts swinging vigorously like the propeller of an aircraft. He might be able to fly if it spins a little bit faster.

t_ch: 哦…哦！这是什么？我的战甲正在以<unbreak>12</unbreak>倍功率运转，感觉好像要飞起来了！
t_en: Oh... Oh? What's happening? My armor is overlocking <unbreak>12</unbreak> times more than its original rate! It feels like I'm going to fly!

t_ch: 是因为开启了推进系统的缘故吗…不对，是因为我好嗨！
t_en: Is it because I've turned on the propulsion system... No, it's because I'm excited!

t_ch: 干脆就这样冲上云霄吧，突破天际！
t_en: Why don't I just soar through the clouds and traverse across the sky like this!

t_ch: 真无聊啊…干什么都提不起劲儿来……
t_en: I'm so bored... Nothing I do seems to get me fired up anymore...

t_ch: 哦？嗨！你看起来…特别酷！这身行头在匹诺康尼很少见啊，可以冒昧问问你是从哪来的吗？
t_en: Oh? Hey! You look really cool! You never see outfits like that around Penacony. May I ask where you are from?

t_ch: 星穹列车是我家！
t_en: The Astral Express is my home!

t_ch: 无名客四海为家。
t_en: For a Nameless, the whole world is my home.

t_ch: 哦哦哦！听起来就不简单——所以那究竟是什么，能给我讲讲吗？
t_en: Ooh! That sounds complicated! So, what does that mean? Can you tell me more?

t_ch: 你简明扼要地为沃尔特解释了何为星穹列车、何为无名客。
t_en: You give Walter a brief explanation of the Astral Express and what it means to be a Nameless.

t_ch: 嗨——这可比电影里演的精彩多了！那你一定去过很多地方吧？一定有过不少惊险刺激的经历吧？
t_en: Hey... That sounds even more amazing than a movie! Then you must have been to many places? You must have had all sorts of dangerous and thrilling experiences.

t_ch: 给我讲讲呗？作为回报，关于匹诺康尼你要是有不了解的，都可以来问我。
t_en: Will you tell me some stories? To repay you, you can come to me any time there is something about Penacony you don't fully understand.

t_ch: 关于匹诺康尼…
t_en: About Penacony...

t_ch: 想问什么尽管问！要说我对这片儿多熟悉——就连艾迪恩公园的地砖有多少块我都一清二楚！
t_en: Ask whatever you like! To say I know a lot about this place is an understatement... I could even tell you how many floor tiles there are in Aideen Park!

t_ch: 所以有多少块？
t_en: So, how many tiles are there?

t_ch: 呃…嗨…这就是个比喻，比喻懂吗！你还是问点实际的吧。
t_en: Uhh... Hey... It's a metaphor. You know what a metaphor is, right? Why don't you ask me a more realistic question?

t_ch: 有什么推荐的游玩项目？
t_en: Are there any fun activities you'd recommend?

t_ch: 附近的话，首选当然是艾迪恩公园！仓鼠球竞速和哈努兄弟角色扮演可都是必玩项目。
t_en: If you mean nearby, my first stop would obviously be Aideen Park! You have to try the hamster ball race and the Brother Hanu roleplay!

t_ch: 说到这个…哈哈，黄金扭蛋机旁边那三个一排的美梦排排乐，你有空可以看去看一眼。
t_en: Speaking of which... Haha, that line of three Dreamy Slots next to the Golden Capsule Machine... if you get a chance, you should go and check them out.

t_ch: 最左边金色那个特别有「个性」，天天劝人戒赌，为此没少被人投诉；但不管被重置多少次它都改不了这个毛病，都快成都市传说了。
t_en: The furthest golden machine to the left has a lot of "character..." Every day it begs and begs people to quit gambling, which has caused a lot of complaints. However, it doesn't matter how many times they reset the machine. This problem never goes away. It's becoming a bit of an urban legend.

t_ch: 至于其它时刻嘛…喜欢自然风光就去「绿洲」、喜欢选秀节目就去「热砂」、想艳遇就去「蓝调」、想购物就去「薄暮」……
t_en: As for the other Moments... If you like natural scenes, check out the Moment of Oasis. If you like talent shows, then go to Moment of Scorchsand. If you want a romantic affair then go to Blue Hour. If you want to go shopping, then go to Moment of Dusk...

t_ch: 要是嫌这些都不够刺激，那我推荐你去「星辰的时刻」——什么球笼、机动球、独轮车，各种比赛那边应有尽有。
t_en: If none of these are exciting enough for you, I suggest heading over to the Moment of Stars... Spheroid, Roboball, Unicycler... they have competitions for everything over there.

t_ch: 有什么推荐的美食？
t_en: Are there any dishes you can recommend?

t_ch: 这个嘛…最有名的苏乐达你肯定喝过了吧？那我推荐你试试噼咔白葡萄汽水，那玩意味道不咋样，但是劲儿特别足。美梦糖浆嘛…我倒是不太推荐，太甜了，而且喝完容易犯困。
t_en: Hmm... SoulGlad is the most popular one. You must have tried that, right? Then I recommend trying Pika White Grape Soda. It may not taste great, but it's got a lot of kick! Dream Syrup on the other hand... I wouldn't recommend it. It's too sweet and makes you really sleepy.

t_ch: 至于吃的…如果是为了打卡的话，肯定得试试钟表披萨和橡木蛋糕卷——虽然那个蛋糕卷嚼着跟啃木头差不多。
t_en: As for food... If you just want to check out some popular foods, you have to try Clockie Pizza and Oak Cake Rolls... Even if eating those cakes is a bit like chewing on wood...

t_ch: 但如果你想吃点实在的，那我推荐飞碟堡和百层圣代。当然，圣代千万别点无糖的！
t_en: But if you want to eat something a bit more filling, I recommend Floatdisc Burgers and Hundred-Layer Sundaes. Of course, whatever you do, don't order the sugar-free sundae!

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 哎呀，这么快，我还没聊够呢……
t_en: Ah, so soon? I still have so much to talk about...

t_ch: 我还有事，回见吧。
t_en: I have things to do. See you around.

t_ch: 行吧。关于匹诺康尼，如果你还有什么想要了解的，尽管来问我就好。
t_en: Alright. If there's anything else you want to know about Penacony, come and ask me anytime.

t_ch: 哦，你来啦？最近有没有什么新鲜事，跟我分享分享呗？
t_en: Oh, you're here? Do you have any new stories to share with me?

t_ch: 这一天天的，我快无聊死了。倒不是说这梦里不好，不过嘛……
t_en: Living day after day like this... I could just die of boredom. I'm not saying that the dream world is bad, it's just...

t_ch: 假如你从出生起就开始喝苏乐达，那这玩意对你而言，不就和白水没什么区别了吗？
t_en: Imagine you've drunk SoulGlad your whole life since you were born. Then, to you, drinking SoulGlad would be no different to just drinking plain old tap water, wouldn't it?

t_ch: 真想来场说走就走的星际冒险啊……
t_en: I wish I could just run away on an interstellar adventure...

t_ch: 他们总说这梦境有多让人流连忘返，但看久了也就是座繁华一点的普通都市而已。
t_en: They all say how the Dreamscape is so amazing that people never want to leave... But after you've stared at it long enough, it's just another slightly more prosperous city.

t_ch: 其实我可羡慕你这样的人了，自由自在、无拘无束，想去哪就去哪，既没有负担也没有顾虑……
t_en: Honestly, I really envy people like you. Completely free, no restraints, going wherever the wind takes you, no burdens or worries...

t_ch: 心动不如行动。
t_en: Why be moved when you can get moving?

t_ch: 你又怎么知道我没行动呢？
t_en: How do you know I haven't gotten moving?

t_ch: 你不能外出吗？
t_en: Can you not leave?

t_ch: 瞧你这话说的…匹诺康尼已经不做监狱很多年了。
t_en: Oh, c'mon... Penacony hasn't been a prison for a long time.

t_ch: 别的不说，光是冒险中可能用到的资源装备，我就已经收藏了整整一屋子。
t_en: I already have a whole room full of resources and equipment that might be useful on an adventure, and that's just the beginning.

t_ch: 更何况…虽然还没离开过阿斯德纳星系，附近的星球我可没少转悠。但没有哪一颗能让我感受到冒险的快乐——
t_en: What's more... Although I've never left the Asdana star system, I have travelled to a lot of the nearby planets. It's just that none of them have really allowed me to feel the joy of adventure...

t_ch: 那些地方充斥着毫无特色的建筑、乏味过时的娱乐、矫揉造作的景观…甚至还不如匹诺康尼呢！
t_en: They're all just filled with unremarkable buildings, outdated entertainment, and artificial scenery... They can't even measure up to Penacony!

t_ch: 真正的冒险，必是充满曲折险阻、惊心动魄，必是能够目睹最真实的宇宙……
t_en: A true adventure needs to be full of twists and turns, thrills and excitement, and allow you to witness the truest universe...

t_ch: 那你要当无名客吗？
t_en: So, you want to be a Nameless?

t_ch: 我可以邀请你上车。
t_en: I can invite you to the Express.

t_ch: 呃…这、多谢你的好意，但现在不行，我不能随随便便答应你。
t_en: Uhh... Umm, thank you for the kind offer, but I cannot right now. I cannot just casually agree to something like that...

t_ch: 星际冒险是严肃的、郑重的，我需要提前做出完备的计划，等待一个完美的时机，这才能表现出我对这件事的重视……
t_en: Interstellar adventures are serious and solemn. I must make flawless preparations and wait for the perfect timing to show how serious I am about this...

t_ch: 他们总说这梦境有多让人流连忘返，但看久了也就是座繁华一点的普通都市而已。
t_en: They all say how the Dreamscape is so amazing that people never want to leave... But after you've stared at it long enough, it's just another slightly more prosperous city.

t_en: Honestly, I really envy people like you. Completely free, no restraints, going wherever the wind takes you, no burdens or worries...

t_ch: 心动不如行动。
t_en: Why be moved when you can get moving?

t_ch: 你又怎么知道我没行动呢？
t_en: How do you know I haven't gotten moving?

t_en: Can you not leave?

t_ch: 瞧你这话说的…匹诺康尼已经不做监狱很多年了。
t_en: Oh, c'mon... Penacony hasn't been a prison for a long time.

t_ch: 别的不说，光是冒险中可能用到的资源装备，我就已经收藏了整整一屋子。
t_en: I already have a whole room full of resources and equipment that might be useful on an adventure, and that's just the beginning.

t_ch: 更何况…虽然还没离开过阿斯德纳星系，附近的星球我可没少转悠。但没有哪一颗能让我感受到冒险的快乐——
t_en: What's more... Although I've never left the Asdana star system, I have travelled to a lot of the nearby planets. It's just that none of them have really allowed me to feel the joy of adventure...

t_en: They're all just filled with unremarkable buildings, outdated entertainment, and artificial scenery... They can't even measure up to Penacony!

t_en: A true adventure needs to be full of twists and turns, thrills and excitement, and allow you to witness the truest universe...

t_ch: 那你要当无名客吗？
t_en: So, you want to be a Nameless?

t_ch: 我可以邀请你上车。
t_en: I can invite you to the Express.

t_ch: 呃…这、多谢你的好意，但现在不行，我不能随随便便答应你。
t_en: Uhh... Umm, thank you for the kind offer, but I cannot right now. I cannot just casually agree to something like that...

t_ch: 星际冒险是严肃的、郑重的，我需要提前做出完备的计划，等待一个完美的时机，这才能表现出我对这件事的重视……
t_en: Interstellar adventures are serious and solemn. I must make flawless preparations and wait for the perfect timing to show how serious I am about this...

t_ch: 果然和那种大老粗玩不来……
t_en: I just knew that I could never spend time with those oafs...

t_ch: 你知道我那俩「结拜兄弟」吗？不知道也没关系，听我说就是了。
t_en: Do you know about my "sworn brothers"? If you don't know, I'll explain anyway.

t_ch: 前些日子我在影城闲逛，心血来潮去门口的快餐车转了转，然后就多了俩兄弟……
t_en: A few days ago, I was wandering around the theme park and went to check out the food trucks by the entrance on a whim. Then, I ended up gaining a couple of brothers...

t_ch: …多了俩什么？
t_en: ...Gaining a couple of what?

t_ch: 买零食送兄弟？
t_en: Do brothers come free with the snacks?

t_ch: …你先听我说完。当时正好有个钟表小子的皮套演员在和老板聊天，我一听，这不巧了——俩人一个叫沃尔普，一个叫沃尔纳。
t_en: Listen to the rest of my story first. At that moment, I happened to overhear a Clockie actor talking to his boss and would you believe it... Their names were Wolpe and Warner!

t_ch: 俗话说得好：「三个沃尔X，顶个钟表匠。」，我们就在钟表小子雕像前结拜了。
t_en: The saying goes: "The more Ws you get, the better." So, we vowed to be brothers in front of the Clockie Statue.

t_ch: 老三沃尔纳倒是还好，人够机灵，还能说会道，相处下来也算舒坦；但那个沃尔普…唉，听说他以前是个银河知名的运动健将，但在我看来完全就是糙人一个！
t_en: The youngest, Warner, wasn't that bad. He was smart enough and quite eloquent. We got along pretty well. But that Wolpe... Ah, I hear he used to be a famous athlete across the cosmos, but I just think he's an oaf!

t_ch: 请他们去钟表餐厅吃个饭吧，别人都细嚼慢咽，就他一个劲儿往嘴里塞，跟打仗似的。
t_en: I figured I'd take them out for a meal at the Clock Diner. But where most people take their time and chew slowly, he just rammed it all in his mouth like he was at war with his food.

t_ch: 最近鸢尾花家系有个小歌手的「纯美之歌」不是有点名气吗，亏我特意带他们去享受一下文化熏陶，结果呢？沃尔普这老小子，听了没一会就睡得和气泡锂犬似的。
t_en: Recently, the Iris Family has that young singer who sang "Song of Beauty," right? She's pretty popular. Well, I had the great idea of taking them to experience some culture, and what happens? That Wolpe fool falls asleep and is snoring like a bubble hound before she's even sung a few bars!

t_ch: 果然偏远小行星出来的…就是上不得台面…也是，要真是个有能耐的，也不至于退役之后来当什么皮套演员了。
t_en: Turns out he's just from some remote minor planet... Not suitable to be seen with in public... I guess that makes sense. If he actually had any skills, why would he retire to become a costumed performer?

t_ch: 果然偏远小行星出来的…就是上不得台面……
t_en: Turns out he's just from some remote minor planet... Not suitable to be seen with in public...

t_ch: 摊上这种「结拜兄弟」，可真是…唉！
t_en: Only I could end up with "sworn brothers" like that... Eugh!

t_ch: 瞧瞧他们，挤破了头都想往这梦里钻……
t_en: Look at them, they're all desperate to get into this dream...

t_ch: 哎呦，怎么竟然还有人相信「在匹诺康尼工作满<unbreak>70</unbreak>年，才能拿到永久居留权」这种事情啊，怪好笑的……
t_en: Ah, how are there people who still believe that "you have to work in Penacony for <unbreak>70</unbreak> years to receive the right to permanent residency" nonsense... It's ridiculous...

t_ch: 明明平时看着挺机灵一个人，怎么在这种事上半点也不灵光，啧啧。
t_en: Some of these people are actually very intelligent. But when it comes to things like this, all sense just completely deserts them, tsk tsk.

t_ch: 不过…倒是不能理解那些「逐梦客」是怎么想的，毕竟是匹诺康尼嘛…哈哈……
t_en: Although, I can understand how these Dreamchasers feel... This is Penacony after all... Haha

t_ch: 更何况，酒店的服务再好，又有谁不希望能在黄金的时刻有套自己的房子呢，对吧？
t_en: What's more, it doesn't matter how amazing the hotel service is here, who wouldn't wish to have their own home in Golden Hour, right?

t_ch: 哎呦，怎么竟然还有人相信「在匹诺康尼工作满<unbreak>70</unbreak>年，才能拿到永久居留权」这种事情啊，怪好笑的……
t_en: Ah, how are there people who still believe that "you have to work in Penacony for <unbreak>70</unbreak> years to receive the right to permanent residency" nonsense... It's ridiculous...

t_ch: 明明平时看着挺机灵一个人，怎么在这种事上半点也不灵光，啧啧。
t_en: Some of these people are actually very intelligent. But when it comes to things like this, all sense just completely deserts them, tsk tsk.

t_ch: 不过…倒是不能理解那些「逐梦客」是怎么想的，毕竟是匹诺康尼嘛…哈哈……
t_en: Although, I can understand how these Dreamchasers feel... This is Penacony after all... Haha

t_ch: 更何况，酒店的服务再好，又有谁不希望能在黄金的时刻有套自己的房子呢，对吧？
t_en: What's more, it doesn't matter how amazing the hotel service is here, who wouldn't wish to have their own home in Golden Hour, right?

t_ch: 欢迎来到美梦小镇，滴答！
t_en: Welcome to Dreamville, tick-tock!

t_ch: 亲爱的朋友，钟表小子为你服务，滴答！
t_en: Dear friend, Clockie is here to serve you today, tick-tock!

t_ch: 你今天怎么怪怪的？
t_en: Why are you acting so strange today?

t_ch: …怪怪的——滴答？这都被你看出来了，我的朋友……
t_en: ...Strange... Tick-tock? I see you noticed, my friend...

t_ch: 钟表小子中了石头老板的诡计，误饮了过期的汽水，滴答——
t_en: Clockie fell for Boss Stone's wicked scheme and accidentally drank an expired soda, tick-tock...

t_ch: 不过别担心，钟表小子很快就会重新振作起来的，滴答！
t_en: But don't worry, Clockie will pull himself together in no time, tick-tock!

t_ch: 你怎么在这…
t_en: Why are you here...

t_ch: 滴答？美梦小镇是钟表小子的家，钟表小子当然会在这里啦！
t_en: Tick-tock? Dreamville is Clockie's home, of course Clockie would be here!

t_ch: 我的朋友，看来你还不太了解这个美丽的小镇吧？
t_en: My friend, I see you still do not fully understand our beautiful little town?

t_ch: 相信我，只要多转转，你总会爱上这里的，滴答！
t_en: Believe me, just take a little stroll around and you will fall in love, tick-tock!

t_ch: 服务？别人又看不到你…
t_en: Serve me? But no one else can see you...

t_ch: …滴答？看、看不到？这…天呐，我的朋友，你是怎么知道的？
t_en: ...Tick-tock? C—Can't see me? This... Oh dear, my friend, how did you know?

t_ch: 我还以为汽水先生的「隐身薄荷汽水」是我的独家机密呢！
t_en: I thought that Mr. Soda's "invisibility mint soda" was a special secret just for me!

t_ch: 善良的朋友啊，你是不会将钟表小子的秘密武器告诉邪恶的石头老板的，对吧？
t_en: My kind friend, you won't tell the evil Boss Stone about Clockie's secret weapon, will you?

t_ch: 很高兴遇见你，亲爱的朋友，滴答！
t_en: I'm happy to see you my dear friend, tick-tock!

t_ch: 愿你在美梦小镇度过一段愉快的时光，滴答——
t_en: I hope you have a wonderful time in Dreamville, tick-tock!

t_ch: 可以和你聊聊吗？
t_en: Can we have a chat?

t_ch: 当然没问题啦，滴答！钟表小子对朋友知无不言，滴答！
t_en: Of course we can, tick-tock! Clockie would tell his friends anything, tick-tock!

t_ch: 聊聊美梦小镇。
t_en: Let's talk about Dreamville.

t_ch: 美梦小镇，滴答！美丽又和平的小镇，滴答！我和我的朋友们在这里快快乐乐、无忧无虑地生活着，滴答！
t_en: Dreamville, tick-tock! A beautiful and peaceful village, tick-tock! My friends and I live happy and carefree lives together here, tick-tock!

t_ch: 然而——可恶的石头老板觊觎着建造小镇的石料，那个大坏蛋竟然自称是小镇主人的后代，想把大家都赶出去，滴答！
t_en: However... The evil Boss Stone covets the stones that we used to build our town. That villain even claims that he is the town master's successor and wants to force us all out of here, tick-tock!

t_ch: 不过请放心，朋友！我和我的伙伴们是不会让他的邪恶阴谋得逞的，滴答！
t_en: But don't you worry, my friend! My friends and I will never let his evil plan succeed, tick-tock!

t_ch: 聊聊你的朋友。
t_en: Let's talk about your friends.

t_ch: 我的朋友们都是非常有趣的家伙——
t_en: All of my friends are endlessly fascinating...

t_ch: 哈努兄弟，我最可靠的伙伴，我永远能放心地将背后交给他，滴答！
t_en: Brother Hanu is my most reliable companion. I can always relax with him watching my back, tick-tock!

t_ch: 汽水先生，小镇上最有「内涵」的家伙，他的汽水总能让我精神百倍，滴答！
t_en: Mr. Soda is the most "cultured" person in town. His sodas always get me brimming with energy, tick-tock!

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师和折纸小鸟们，他们建造了这座小镇，就像爱护自己的巢一样爱护着它，滴答！
t_en: Professor Owl and the origami birds... they created this town and they care for it like they would their own nests, tick-tock!

t_ch: 还有音符小姐、镜子公主、木头老爷、仓鼠球骑士…相信我，朋友！你也会像我一样喜欢他们的，滴答！
t_en: Then there is Miss Note, Mirror Princess, Old Man Wood, Hamster Ball Knight... Trust me, my friend! I know you'll love them all just like I do, tick-tock!

t_ch: 就先聊到这吧。
t_en: Let's stop here for now.

t_ch: 没问题，滴答！
t_en: No worries, tick-tock!

t_ch: 有空再见！
t_en: See you again soon!

t_ch: 请记住，我的朋友！无论何时，钟表小子为你服务，滴答！
t_en: Remember, my dear friend! Clockie is here to serve you anytime!

t_ch: 欢迎来到美梦小镇，滴答！
t_en: Welcome to Dreamville, tick-tock!

t_ch: 亲爱的朋友，钟表小子为你服务，滴答！
t_en: Dear friend, Clockie is here to serve you today, tick-tock!

t_ch: 想要和我一起逛逛美梦小镇吗，滴答！
t_en: Do you want to walk around Dreamville with me, tick-tock!

t_ch: 一阵沉默后，眼前的「钟表小子」突然变为一个形容憔悴的男人。
t_en: After a moment of silence, the "Clockie" before you suddenly transforms into a haggard-looking man.

t_ch: 呼…真的非常抱歉，但我现在感觉不太好，可以让我稍微歇一会儿吗？
t_en: Phew... I'm so sorry. I don't feel very well. Could you give me a moment to rest?

t_ch: 当然没问题。
t_en: Of course!

t_ch: 哈啊…谢谢你的体贴……
t_en: Ahh... Thank you for your kindness...

t_ch: 好家伙，当场下班是吧？
t_en: You're just clocking off right now, huh?

t_ch: 抱歉…我知道这样非常不负责，但现在，我只想透一口气……
t_en: Sorry... I know this is extremely unprofessional but... I just need... to breathe for a moment...

t_ch: 疲惫的男人再次陷入沉默，表情匮乏，目光涣散。
t_en: The exhausted man falls silent again, his expression empty and his eyes unfocused.

t_ch: 或许是他太过难看的面色着实令人同情、也或许是他的颓丧感染了你，你竟突然想和他聊聊。
t_en: For some reason, you find yourself wanting to talk to him. Maybe his pitiable expression has inspired some kind of sympathy in you, or maybe his depression is infectious.

t_ch: 于是你走近了些，轻轻拍了拍他垮塌的肩膀。
t_en: So, you approach him and gently pat his slumped shoulders.

t_ch: 啊？哦，抱、抱歉，我没注意到…你是想和卡通角色互动吗，那边还有其他演员……
t_en: Huh? Oh, s—sorry, I didn't notice you... Did you want to interact with one of the characters? There are more actors over there...

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 需要帮忙吗？
t_en: Do you need help?

t_ch: 哈哈…你想听我抱怨吗？唉…既然你到现在还没走，我就当你想听啦。
t_en: Haha... Do you want to hear me whinge? Ah... Since you're still here, I'll take that as a yes.

t_ch: 我这人，怎么说呢…嗨，我知道他们都觉得我傻…毕竟大概没谁都到这把年纪，还以为人能靠梦想活着了吧。
t_en: A man like me, well... Ah, how do I put this? I know they all think I'm stupid... I mean, no one else at my age still thinks that people can rely on their dreams to make a living, right?

t_ch: …当初…退役之后，我没按他们说的，靠过去的名声接广告赚流量，而是为了最喜欢的钟表小子，跑来匹诺康尼当皮套演员。
t_en: At first... After I retired, I didn't listen to them and didn't use my fame to live off of advertising deals. Instead, I came running straight to Penacony to be a costumed performer for my favorite Clockie.

t_ch: 结果这份工作根本就不是我想象中的样子…更糟糕的是，酒店的房费消耗了我大半身家，而如今的身体状况又不允许我重返竞技赛场……
t_en: In the end, this job was nothing like I imagined... What's worse, the hotel fees have used up the majority of my savings, and now my body is in no state to get back into the competitive arena...

t_ch: 要问「后悔吗」——我自己也不知道，哈哈。我只知道如今的我就像是失去了汽水先生的钟表小子，再提不起半分精神、点不燃一星热情……
t_en: If you ask me "Do you regret it?" ...Even I'm not sure, haha. All I know is that, right now, I feel like Clockie without Mr. Soda. I can't get my spirits up, or ignite even a spark of enthusiasm...

t_ch: 哎呀，自顾自说了这么多，你都听烦了吧。谢谢你听我抱怨，我现在感觉…好多了。
t_en: Ah, I've ranted for so long, you must be tired of listening to all this. Thank you for listening to my complaints. I actually feel... a lot better now.

t_ch: 短暂的沉默之后，「钟表小子」又出现在你的面前。
t_en: After a brief silence, Clockie appears before your eyes once more.

t_ch: 亲爱的朋友，钟表小子为你服务，滴答！
t_en: Dear friend, Clockie is here to serve you today, tick-tock!

t_ch: 星际冒险是严肃的、郑重的，我需要提前做出完备的计划，等待一个完美的时机，这才能表现出我对这件事的重视……
t_en: Interstellar adventures are serious and solemn. I must make flawless preparations and wait for the perfect timing to show how serious I am about this...

t_ch: 亲爱的朋友，钟表小子为你服务，滴……
t_en: My dear friend, Clockie is here to serve you, tick...

t_ch: 抱歉，我想稍微休息一会，就一会……
t_en: Sorry, I just need to take a quick rest, just a quick one...

t_ch: 美梦小镇是个美丽又和平…个鬼啊！
t_en: Dreamville really is a beautiful and peaceful... Ah, who am I kidding!

t_ch: 我真是——受、够、了！！！
t_en: I'VE HAD IT!!!

t_ch: 眼前的「钟表小子」哼哧哼哧喘着粗气，扁圆表盘与细瘦双腿紧绷成两点半钟的折角，沉重的「时针」在前后一刻度区间内来回晃动着。
t_en: The Clockie before your eyes pants and breathes heavily. The angle of their thin, folded legs against the circular clock face shows half-past two, as the heavy hour hand sways back and forth within a quarter-hour interval.

t_ch: 那群——狗东西！自己什么活也不干，一天到晚就知道对着别人指手画脚！
t_en: Those... scum! They never do any work themselves. All they know how to do is give orders!

t_ch: 动不动就「自愿加班」、动不动就「扣半年绩效」…我们每天从早干到晚，到手那点苜蓿币扣完酒店房费连块钟表披萨都买不起！
t_en: Every other day it's "voluntary overtime" or "we're cutting the half-year performance bonus"... We're here daily from dawn until dusk and the meager Alfalfa credits they pay us aren't even enough for a slice of Clockie Pizza after I've paid my hotel fees!

t_ch: 我们是喜欢钟表小子、是可以为爱发电，但这不意味着我们就甘愿被压榨！就连石头老板做坏事之前都知道找个「体面的理由」呢！
t_en: We all love Clockie, but just because we're passionate about it doesn't mean that we're okay with being exploited! Even Boss Stone knows you can't just be evil without a solid reason!

t_ch: 可恶啊…动画…动画不应该是给人带来快乐的东西吗？
t_en: Dagnabbit... Aren't... Aren't cartoons supposed to bring people joy?

t_ch: 那为什么来到了「美梦小镇」之后，我反而再也快乐不起来了呢……
t_en: Then why is it that ever since I came to Dreamville, I've lost all my happiness?

t_ch: 他们永远是我最好的朋友，滴答！
t_en: They will always be my best friends, tick-tock!

t_ch: 钟表小子为你服务，滴答！你想听我介绍一下我的朋友们吗，滴答！
t_en: Clockie is here to serve you, tick-tock! Would you like to hear me introduce my friends, tick-tock?

t_ch: 哈努兄弟，最可靠的老大哥，滴答！他不仅带我和汽水先生去钟表餐厅吃大餐，还邀请我们去大剧院听音乐会，滴答！
t_en: Brother Hanu is my most reliable companion, tick-tock! Not only did he take me and Mr. Soda to Clock Diner for a feast, he even invited us to the Grand Theater for a recital, tick-tock!

t_ch: 虽然我听不懂那么高雅的东西，还半路睡着了，哈哈！但我真的非常高兴，滴答！
t_en: Although, I don't really understand such elegant things and I fell asleep halfway through, haha! But, I was really happy, tick-tock!

t_ch: 汽水先生，我的好朋友，滴答！他又勤劳又机灵，人也特别善良，每次有卖不完的快餐都会留给我，还不要我的钱，滴答！
t_en: Mr. Soda is my good friend, tick-tock! He is hard-working, intelligent, and really kind. Whenever he has leftovers, he leaves them for me and doesn't ask me for money, tick-tock!

t_ch: 能和他们成为兄弟真是太好了，滴答！
t_en: I'm so happy I became brothers with them, tick-tock!

t_ch: 我还有很多其他好朋友，他们都是很好的人…有机会我再向你好好介绍他们吧？
t_en: I have lots of other good friends, too! They are all wonderful people... I'll introduce you to them all when I get an opportunity, okay?

t_ch: 喏，这是汽水先生给我的苏乐达，也分你一瓶吧，滴答！
t_en: Oh, Mr. Soda gave me these SoulGlad, I'll share one with you, too, tick-tock!

t_ch: 钟表小子为你服务，滴答！你想听我介绍一下我的朋友们吗，滴答！
t_en: Clockie is here to serve you, tick-tock! Would you like to hear me introduce my friends, tick-tock?

t_ch: 哈努兄弟，最可靠的老大哥，滴答！他不仅带我和汽水先生去钟表餐厅吃大餐，还邀请我们去大剧院听音乐会，滴答！
t_en: Brother Hanu is my most reliable companion, tick-tock! Not only did he take me and Mr. Soda to Clock Diner for a feast, he even invited us to the Grand Theater for a recital, tick-tock!

t_ch: 虽然我听不懂那么高雅的东西，还半路睡着了，哈哈！但我真的非常高兴，滴答！
t_en: Although, I don't really understand such elegant things and I fell asleep halfway through, haha! But, I was really happy, tick-tock!

t_ch: 汽水先生，我的好朋友，滴答！他又勤劳又机灵，人也特别善良，每次有卖不完的快餐都会留给我，还不要我的钱，滴答！
t_en: Mr. Soda is my good friend, tick-tock! He is hard-working, intelligent, and really kind. Whenever he has leftovers, he leaves them for me and doesn't ask me for money, tick-tock!

t_ch: 能和他们成为兄弟真是太好了，滴答！
t_en: I'm so happy I became brothers with them, tick-tock!

t_ch: 我还有很多其他好朋友，他们都是很好的人…有机会我再向你好好介绍他们吧？
t_en: I have lots of other good friends, too! They are all wonderful people... I'll introduce you to them all when I get an opportunity, okay?

t_ch: 故乡是个很神奇的地方。你出门的时间越长，逃得越远，反而在情感上才离它更近。
t_en: A home is a mysterious thing. The longer you leave it and the farther you roam, the closer you feel to it.

t_ch: 起初，故乡是个很具体的词，一幢房子，也可以是一颗星球。随着时间流逝，它会变得越来越模糊，几个你还叫得出名字的人，小时候没吃到的那颗糖…都可以是故乡。
t_en: At first, home is a word with a very clear meaning. It could be a house, or it could be a planet. But, as time goes on, the meaning becomes more and more blurred. It could be those friends whose names you still remember, or that sweet you never managed to have as a child... They can all be your home.

t_ch: 当你尝试用些固定的形容去描述它的时候，你就开始在失去它了。
t_en: It's precisely when you try to use a fixed definition to describe a home that you begin to lose it.

t_ch: …那你现在呢？
t_en: ...So, how do you feel now about home?

t_ch: 你想念故乡么？
t_en: Are you missing your home?

t_ch: …我？哈哈，我已经在厌恶它了，因为我回到了这里。
t_en: ...Me? Haha, I already hate my home... because now I'm back here.

t_ch: 你是来匹诺康尼游玩的宾客？待的时间不短了吧，告诉我，你喜欢这个地方吗？
t_en: You're a visitor to Penacony, aren't you? You've been here for some time. Tell me, do you like Penacony?

t_ch: 非常好匹诺康尼。
t_en: Penacony's great.

t_ch: …体验非常地差。
t_en: ...I hate it here.

t_ch: 是么？不管好与坏，这里都是我没法离开的地方。
t_en: Really? Well, love it or hate it, I cannot leave this place.

t_ch: 我对钟表小子毫无感觉，但在星际漂泊的漫长旅程中，我依旧会选择重看这部动画来对抗无止境的空虚与孤独，那种温暖的熟悉感…抵得过任何实际的陪伴。
t_en: I have no feelings towards Clockie, but throughout my long days of interstellar travel, I always chose to rewatch that cartoon to stave off the infinite emptiness and loneliness. That warm sense of familiarity... It's as good as any actual companionship.

t_ch: 你是匹诺康尼本地人么？
t_en: Are you a local Penaconian?

t_ch: 是啊。我对这里熟悉得不能再熟悉了，就连衣服都浸透了苏乐达的香味，就连影城展示的所有角色，我都能细致说出它们每个人的来历和故事。
t_en: I am. I could not be any more familiar with Penacony. Even my clothes are saturated with the smell of SoulGlad. Show me any character on the theme park display and I can tell you all of their backgrounds and stories in detail.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼是个好地方，尤其对你们这些宾客而言，热闹，好玩，无休无止，可以肆意放纵直到天亮，不用担心任何现实的烦扰。
t_en: Penacony is a good place, especially for you guests. It's lively, fun, and never stops. You can party until the break of dawn without having to worry about any real-life problems.

t_ch: 但对我来说并非如此，因为它是我的故乡。
t_en: But that is not the case for me, because this is my home.

t_ch: 有机会再聊吧。
t_en: Let's talk another time.

t_ch: 说得有点多了。祝你在影城玩得开心，陌生人。
t_en: I have talked a bit too much. I hope you have a wonderful time in the theme park, stranger.

t_ch: 故乡是个很神奇的地方。你出门的时间越长，逃得越远，反而在情感上才离它更近。
t_en: A home is a mysterious thing. The longer you leave it and the farther you roam, the closer you feel to it.

t_ch: 无论是否喜欢匹诺康尼，这里都是我无法离开的地方……
t_en: Whether you like Penacony or not, this is a place that I just can't leave.

t_ch: 哈，才刚回来不久，我就已经开始在厌恶它了。
t_en: Ha, I've just gotten back, and I've already started to hate it.

t_ch: 我讨厌故乡，因为它知道所有你那些不愿再回想起的往事。
t_en: I hate coming home. It knows all of those things that you don't want to remember.

t_ch: 果然啊，回到了熟悉的地方，过去那些悲伤的难堪的回忆就全部涌了上来。
t_en: It always happens, you get back to a familiar place and all of those sad and painful memories come flooding back.

t_ch: …我有时会想，为什么我的父母偏偏是家族成员？为什么我从小就得学习「谐乐颂」，背诵那些其实根本就没人在乎的词句，只为了讨所谓的大人物开心？
t_en: ...I sometimes think, why did my parents have to be members of The Family? Why did I have to study the "Odes of Harmony" from such a young age? Memorizing and reciting all those words that no one cares about just to please these so-called "important figures"?

t_ch: 又为什么…我明明背得那么出色，最后还是没能挽救我的家庭呢？
t_en: And why... Even though I learned them so well, was I not able to save my family?

t_ch: 看着原本骄傲的父亲，沦落到必须看人脸色行事的地步，我实在无法忍受这种生活的落差，赌气离开了匹诺康尼，再也没和任何人联系过。
t_en: Seeing my once-proud father being reduced to being subservient to others, I could no longer deal with our sudden fall from grace. So, I left Penacony selfishly and never contacted anyone again.

t_ch: 哈…现在我也分不清楚了——我没法忍受的究竟是家庭的巨变，还是当时那个没用的自己？
t_en: Ah... Now, I can't quite tell... Was it the changes in my family that I could not stand, or the fact that I was so powerless?

t_ch: 果然啊，回到了熟悉的地方，过去那些悲伤的难堪的回忆就全部涌了上来。
t_en: It always happens, you get back to a familiar place and all of those sad and painful memories come flooding back.

t_ch: …我有时会想，为什么我的父母偏偏是家族成员？为什么我从小就得学习「谐乐颂」，背诵那些其实根本就没人在乎的词句，只为了讨所谓的大人物开心？
t_en: ...I sometimes think, why did my parents have to be members of The Family? Why did I have to study the "Odes of Harmony" from such a young age? Memorizing and reciting all those words that no one cares about just to please these so-called "important figures"?

t_ch: 又为什么…我明明背得那么出色，最后还是没能挽救我的家庭呢？
t_en: And why... Even though I learned them so well, was I not able to save my family?

t_ch: 看着原本骄傲的父亲，沦落到必须看人脸色行事的地步，我实在无法忍受这种生活的落差，赌气离开了匹诺康尼，再也没和任何人联系过。
t_en: Seeing my once-proud father being reduced to being subservient to others, I could no longer deal with our sudden fall from grace. So, I left Penacony selfishly and never contacted anyone again.

t_ch: 哈…现在我也分不清楚了——我没法忍受的究竟是家庭的巨变，还是当时那个没用的自己？
t_en: Ah... Now, I can't quite tell... Was it the changes in my family that I could not stand, or the fact that I was so powerless?

t_ch: 我从来不认为自己很聪明，但比我蠢的人实在太多了。
t_en: I have never thought that I was that intelligent, but there are far too many people who are more foolish than I am.

t_ch: 你肯定没想过吧，欺骗这个世界有多么容易。
t_en: I'm sure you've never thought how easy it would be to fool this world, have you?

t_ch: 信息技术越是发达，可操作的空间就越大。想完美假扮成另一个人非常困难，但合成对方的声音、仿造电子证明、盗取生物数据都极为简单。
t_en: As information technology grows more developed, the extent that we can manipulate the world grows. Perfectly imitating someone else is difficult, but synthesizing their voice, forging their electronic certificates, and stealing their biological data is extremely easy.
